reviews.review_description: I’m giving it 5 stars because we need to be more turn-based RPGs in VR, but it does need more locomotion options like teleportation or normal smooth locomotion with snap turning. This stepped movement makes me really sick. This should be the highest priority update for the developer to focus on.

This game is exactly what I was looking for! On open-movement based world with collectible items, weapons, stats, and abilities! Unlike anything else on the Oculus Go store, and I feel like I have been waiting for a game of this nature since I purchased my Go! In one day I have already played for 2 hours which is the longest I have played any game continuously in VR. A true step towards the future of MMORPG gaming on VR consoles. The simple, yet intuitive user interface of this game really ties together what would already be a splendid exploration game. The battling mechanisms, shop, and interactions are very nostalgic and bring back the unstoppable addiction I experience when playing Pokémon games for the very first time. Unique collection of enemys, side-quests, and a whole lot of Easter eggs make this world feel like a place I could spend days at a time on. I would love to see more decoration of the areas! Ground plants, flowers, different types of trees, and other small details would really bring this game to another level. I would also love to be able to see monsters before interacting with them as we do with bosses. Maybe some kind of path-finding algorithm to allows them to wander in their respective zones to make traveling more dynamic. A shorter waiting screen would be nice inverterem battles. I literally cannot wait to see what amazing updates come from this game! Very worth the buy, and looking forward to new content!
Game looks simple but there is some charm behind it very fun. Good old school RPG

reviews.review_title: A fun and charming little RPG
reviews.review_description: The Knight of Queen is well made and really captures the feel of classic RPG's. The visuals are simple but very nice, music is catchy, controls are effective and work very well. I am really enjoying it. RPG fans should definitely check it out.

This game is really good! I like straightforward games with relatively limited graphics and this fits the bill perfectly for me. It's clearly inspired by RPG games from the old days but it doesn't feel as limited. The controls are intuitive, the music is catchy and the battle sequences remind me of the old Pokemon games of my youth. The story is easy to understand, the characters are fun and the environments are interesting to look at and explore. This is one of the better games I've found on the Go and it feels really well put-together.

Two very minor niggles, however; the footstep sound effects are as loud as the music and it can get repetitive. It would be nice to have a settings menu to be able to turn down the sound effects. Also the movement through the world is a bit jumpy. It's nothing major, but smooth acceleration would feel a better fit (it could just be my preference). Overall a really fun game and well worth checking out!
